title: Audio Transmitter Receiver TV Computer Speaker Car Adapter 3.5mm Bluetooth 5.0 2-in-1 for Home Sound System/TV/PC/Tablet Headphone General (Black) (Large)
description:
Switch mode:
(1) RX: Receive mode, indicator: blue LED  (2) TX: Transmit mode, indicator light: red LED
RX mode:
Pairing name: KN321, find KN321 in the Bluetooth settings of the phone to pair. The blue light flashes twice a second during the pairing process, and the blue light flashes once every three seconds after the pairing is successful.
how to use:
(1) Plug one end of the audio cable into the audio input of the speaker and the other end into the audio of the KN321.  (2)confirm that it is in RX mode (blue light flash). Find the pairing name in the Bluetooth settings of the phone: KN321 click to pair.
TX mode:
TX mode will automatically search for other Bluetooth receiving devices (such as Bluetooth headset, Bluetooth speaker, Bluetooth receiver, etc.), The red light flashes twice a second during the pairing process, and the red light flashes once every three seconds after the pairing is successful.
How to use:
(1)Plug one end of the audio cable into the audio output of the devices and the other end into the audio of the KN321.  (2)Confirm that it is in TX mode (red light flash). The KN321 will automatically search for Bluetooth headset or Bluetooth speaker pairing.
features: ★[BLUETOOTH TRANSMITTER AND RECEIVER 2IN1] Receiver mode: wirelessly streams audio from your Bluetooth-enabled phone, tablet, or computer to your wired stereo, speakers, or headphones (Only support to be paired with one Bluetooth receiver). Transmitter mode: wirelessly streams audio from your non-Bluetooth TV, home stereo system, or CD player to your Bluetooth headphones or speakers. Switch between transmitter and receiver modes with the simple flick of a switch.
★[NO DRIVER REQUIRED PLUG READY TO USE] No need to download driver, plug and play TV computer or Bluetooth speaker headset.

★[PORTABLE AUDIO-STREAMING POWER] Bluetooth Adapter has got powerful wireless audio streaming capabilities. It features Bluetooth 5.0 chip to ensure high fidelity transmission of Bluetooth audio signal for music streaming to those speakers or home stereo systems that doesn’t have the capability.Slip it into your backpack or laptop bag and take it on-the-go without the slightest effort.

★[WIDE APPLICATION] With the included 3.5mm cable and 3.5mm to 2RCA cable, this Receiver Transmitter adapter can widely be used on your computer, laptop, home stereo system, headphone, smartphone, Mp3 player, CD player, Cars with AUX-in and 3.5mm connector/ Computer Speakers/ Odinary Speakers/ Subwoofers/ Radios/ Multimedia Speakers/ Power Amplifiers/ Echo Walls, etc.

★[QUALITY ASSURANCE AND SERVICE] Provides a 60-day return and 12-month replacement warranty, as well as lifetime accessible technical support. Please contact us if you have any questions. Reply to your message within 12 hours.

reviews.title: Great quality device if you're on a budget
reviews.text: [[VIDEOID:e49e95641bb1dc426258e889516d11e0]] Over great product for the price. I can't complain. If you've got the money and would like to go for a more "high end" product there are plenty of options available. However, this one appeals greater to the general public. Most reviews say the same thing: It cuts out if too far away and it has a short battery life. Also if it is being used while charging you'll heard a little buzzing noise. You get what you pay for. It does its job and I can't complain  for the price.

reviews.title: For A/V wireless solution, it is workable but not perfect.
reviews.text:
First, my purpose is to stream audio to wired QC headphones from my AV receiver, as a replacement for my broken analog FM TV sound transmitter/phones. For THIS purpose... I bought a pair of these BT devices, and it works, but the sync between audio and video is - versus analog - perceptible. The streaming generation is less sensitive to this, but AV-philes find it annoying.

It is a problem in all BT, compressed audio, technologies - to some degree. Compressed audio requires buffering, buffering introduces some minimum delay, and the delay can be worse depending upon the nature of the audio.
For many programs (sports, etc) sync isn't a problem, but in close-up talky-talky scenes the audio lag is annoying vs. the FM phones, which have imperceptible lag. For NON-SYNCed applications - this is a non-issue.
I wish Roku had a AV sync setting (+/- ms). This is what BT 5.0 does - it delays the video based on the estimated audio lag. *But that doesn't help here because these devices do NOT control the video stream*.

Otherwise, audio quality of BT vs FM is equivalent. My FM phones were very good (AR, $150) and audio was excellent, so the BT pair has good sound in this application. I have not noted any static others reported, except for a slight hum when the RX is not connected. Generally, the BT audio is equivalent to any other BT audio connection (ie., streaming iPhone to car stereo, etc.).
8/18/2020 Revision: After a power outage, I noticed a VERY bad hum. Removed RX side's power, no change. Removed TX sides power (from USB outlet on cable box), NO HUM. I tried several other USB outlets, each had some hum though with different qualities. Finally, I got out a good high amp USB hub & used it to power the TX side - no hum at all. SO... the transmitting side is VERY sensitive to ripple in its USB power circuitry.

One VERY annoying thing - my devices both power off after 4hrs (6hrs?) regardless of input, even though both TX/RX devices are permanently wired to USB power. It ought to power down only if on battery or unconnected for some time (firmware update perhaps?). Other than this obvious design choice, I have had no connection issues - power on TX, then RX, connects reliably in ~10s. Once connected stays reliably connected UNTIL it powers down.

So the good: Cheap solution to use superior wired headphones. Small & USB powered, so hides away easily on both TX and RX sides. Cheaper & more versatile than quality FM TV transmitter/phone combos.

And the bad: Audio LAGS behind video noticeably, 50-150ms. Also, I haven't tried with multiple BT sources - but there is no UI, no way to select a specific source - I suspect this would be a problem for some.

Overall: For the price, I can't complain and wouldn't spend 5x for good FM TV audio phones.

reviews.title: Compatible With All Devices
reviews.text: Amazing product! I like the easy connectivity and sound quality is top of the line. I wish had known about this product before. It is compatible with all of my devices. Even my older television! Five stars all the way!

reviews.title: Nope. Didn't work with TV..
reviews.text: I bought this to try to move the sound FROM my Panasonic, non-bluetooth television TO my bluetooth headphones or a bluetooth speaker. Tried both, neither worked - was a complete waste of time. Now, who's going to refund me for the printer ink to print a label to return it?

reviews.title: Works like a charm
reviews.text: Fantastic. Works right out of the box. I set it up with an aux to an older stereo and it connected right away. I'm probably going to buy another one for the office.

reviews.title: For my son’s gaming headset...
reviews.text: This little contraption is quite awesome. Bought it for the headset my son uses while playing video games. Was tired of finding his headset left on the floor, so attached this to his entertainment center and now he hangs his $100 headset on this (up off the floor where it won’t get stepped on/ damaged).  Solid construction. Easy install (no holes, no tools/ other hardware required).

reviews.title: Great purchase
reviews.text: It was just a tad bit too small for where I wanted it,  but I found the perfect spot on the printer stand next to the desk. Very well made.  Not just a cheap piece of plastic.  Strong and sturdy. Folds up out of the way when not in use.

reviews.title: This Gadget is Great!
reviews.text: Love this thing! Put it under my desk to hold my purse. How easy can it be?! Slide it on. Tighten it securely. Add purse. Done and done! Nice quality, too…not cheesy or flimsy.

reviews.title: Great and well made
reviews.text: This thing is great and well made from metal.  This is my second one.  It cost more than the plastics ones, but it is very well made.  The holder folds up once you have it clamped to a desk to shelf.  It is strong and supports heavy headphones well.  It is a little expensive but well worth the investment.

title: 1MORE ComfoBuds Mini Hybrid Active Noise Cancelling Earbuds, in-Ear Headphones with Stereo Sound, Bluetooth 5.2 Headset with 4 Mics, Clear Calls, Wireless Charging, Soothing Sound, Waterproof, White
features: True Quiet in a Small Package: We put 1MORE’s proprietary QuietMax active noise cancellation technology into the compact body of the ComfoBuds Mini to provide true quiet. With up to 40dB noise cancellation depth, you can get a more immersive listening experience. And the earbuds allow you to switch between multiple listening modes to fit different scenarios.

Smaller Size, Increased Comfort: The ComfoBuds Mini is the world’s smallest and lightest ANC earbuds. A single earbud weighs only 3.7g, and with the extra tiny size and ergonomic design, you’ll get an “invisible” but super comfortable wearing experience. Four sizes of silicone ear tips are provided to get the perfect fit for your ear and better audio performance.

Tiny Mics, Crystal-Clear Calls: Two mics hidden in the tiny body of earbud work together to filter out background noise, then accurately pick up your voice and amplify it, keeping your voice sounding clear, even when calling in a carnival parade.

Astounding Audio Performance: Tiny in size, but no compromise on sound quality. A 7mm dynamic driver produces a rich, powerful, authentic sound that is usually reserved for big bulky headphones. And the integration of SoundID technology allows you to get your perfect sound that fits your ears and preference.
Convenient Wireless Charging: The ComfoBuds Mini TWS earbuds can power up super easily on the go with Qi-compatible wireless charging, say goodbye to tangled cables.

reviews.title: Mini, but Mighty
reviews.text:
Look, we know you aren't here for premium sound quality if you're looking at the tiniest earbuds and in this price range. That being said, these do still sound quite nice! There's a lot of oomph for such a small package. Unless you're used to professional grade sound equipment, these will cover most anyone's sound needs.

SMALL EAR HAVERS! I have been on a quest to find comfy earbuds. So far, these have come the closest. I can wear them for like 2 hours with minimal discomfort. This is a huge upgrade from "most cause physical pain near immediately". When placed correctly, they're pretty solid. I couldn't shake them out. They did come loose a handful of times and needed to be readjusted, but in comparison to the others needing that every 5 minutes? I'll take it.

They say they have ANC, but honestly, I'm not so sure. In my personal experience, turning ANC on didn't actually cancel any noise. It just brought the bass out. Unless I just can't get the right fit, the ANC mode is effectively just a "make the music sound better" button.

The drawback here is that you can only use ANC if both buds are in. Not usually a problem, unless you want to pop one in to listen to music and leave the other ear open for communication. The make the music sound better button won't do anything with just one bud.

reviews.title: Basically perfect, fantastic wind ANC, perfect case
reviews.text:
I needed a replacement for my Galaxy buds pro's that have the fatal ANC "roar in one ear" that Samsung won't fix, and they don't stay in the ear. Bring an absolutely ENORMOUS fan of the 1more comfobuds z (the single best sleep focused earbuds at any price) I liked that these are also small, the case shape is wonderful (flip top, tiny, wireless charger) and they're relatively inexpensive.

The sound quality uses 1more's app to do a "sounds" hearing preference test. I would prefer a proper EQ option but it works well enough. I prefer real audiophile iem's and headphones, but enjoyed the Galaxy buds on bass boost, they almost hit like planar magnetics. These are about 80% there. They're totally serviceable, with the only standout being a pretty good technical performance, with genuinely solid imaging and separation. They absolutely do need the soundID set up though, I hate the stock tuning. For tiny little things they are, I am perfectly happy with the sound quality when I want music on my bike or public transit.

The ANC is stellar. They have a specific mode for wind reduction that downright curbstomps Samsung and apple. If you ride a bike, you need 1more ANC buds. The transparency mode is great too, very natural sounding. It doesn't have that "talk and it switches to transparency" gimmick, but I found it obnoxious on Samsung.

Touch controls are easily customized and no "single touch" feature means you don't accidentally press anything readjusting or putting them in. Auto pauses when you take one out, your can use just one or the other no problem

The app features depend on the product but these are meant to be usable for sleeping, do they come preloaded with a library of sleep/ambient sounds. I just use my own but it's a nice feature. I can easily fall asleep with them in and I'm a very picky side sleeper, but I do prefer the absolutely microscopic comfobuds z's.

The stock eartips did bother me a bit, they have a little ridge from the mold that irritated my ears after a few hours of use. They have a very odd mounting system that I was only able to find one option for, spinfit 1025 tips are shallow enough to fit and still let the case close (the wells for the eartips is a bit to shallow, id like it if they addressed this on a revision) so if you don't like the tips, spinfit 1025's.

The case magnets that hold the buds in are hilariously strong. Your have to SMACK it to dislodge them. Very nice detail. The case size is tiny and perfectly shaped to slide into pockets, with a flip top that you can open one handed

For being so small they also stay put well. Not perfect, but I can work out or mow the lawn, an impossible task of the Galaxy buds pro.

Overall though, these things rock. They're exactly what I need as a paramedic who also rides a bicycle. 1more is a serious brand with some seriously well thought out products at extremely competitive prices. I can sleep on my side with a podcast and still be able to wake up for a call, they crush wind nose when I'm riding my bike, they sound perfectly adequately tuned with impressive imaging (once you run the sound id feature once) and the case is (slightly too shallow recessions for the eartips aside) very nice in the hand and perfectly shaped.

Highly recommend.

reviews.title: Bought this twice- love it
reviews.text:
I bought a pair of these about 4 ish months ago and they’re great - a terrific value for the price point.

2 weeks ago the earbuds disappeared into the ether (thanks cats) and they needed to be replaced-I explored getting something different but the Comfobuds minis check all the boxes.

The only drawback is you have to download the app to your phone and your earbuds need to be paired to your phone in order for you to check the power levels of the earbuds and the charging case.

reviews.title: Great quality, great price
reviews.text: I have a pair of Apple AirPods Pro, which I like well enough.  I bought these after reading a review somewhere.  I love them.  The sound quality and noise cancellation seem to work as well as the Air Pods, and they actually seem to stay in place better.  I’d definitely buy them again.

reviews.title: No more 1More
reviews.text: I lost the power in the right earbud after 10 min... I had charged them for 1 hour before connecting them to my phone. right ear bud would not sync after 10 min . I did everything recommended to correct the problem What a waste of my time. Sent them back to Amazon no more 1More!!!!!!

title: USB C Hub, Baseus 6-in-1 Portable USB C Adapter for iPad Pro, Upgrade Type C Dongle to 4K HDMI, USB 3.0, SD/Micro SD Card Reader, 3.5mm Earphone Jack, Type C PD Charger, Independent Switch
description:
It easily extends the USB-C port of your laptop to 4K HDMI, 2 USB 3.0 Port, TF Card Reader, fast USB-C PD charging port and 3.5mm Headphone Jack. Plug & Play, with no additional drivers or installation necessary.
Features:
Turns a single USB-C connection into 6 ports (3.5mm Audio Jack, 2 USB 3.0, Micro SD, USB-C, HDMI) Take full advantage of iPadOS with the hub that supports both USB-C and USB-A flash/hard storage drives. Connect to USB-A peripherals via high speed USB 3.1 Gen 1 capable of 5Gb/s transfer speed. Use hub and charge iPad Pro at the same time! Also supports USB-C data. Simply connect with the HDMI port, mirrors your laptop's display (supports 4K at 30Hz ) on any HDMI-enabled device. Dual USB 3.0 ports, transmission rate up to 5Gbps, can transfer a high-definition movie in seconds. 3.5mm Audio and Mic 2 in 1 Port makes listening, talking, or recording a breeze. Built in TF slot for easy access to files from universal Micro SD Memory Card(SDXC: with high storage capacity up to 2TB)
Specification:
Material: Aluminum alloy Colour: Deep gray Input:Dual Type-C  Output: USB3.0*2+HDMI*1+PD*1+3.5mm*1+TF*1 USB3.0 transmission speed: 5Gbps HDMI Resolution: 4K@30Hz  PD input power: 100W Max.  TF Card: Supports data retrieval from 2TB Memory Card  3.5mm Audio Frequency Port: Port supports standard US headphone sets Size: 83*36*19mm Weight: 65g
Package List:
1* USB C Hub Adapter 1* User Manual
features: Creative Mobile Hub - The Newest Version 6-in-1 USB-C Hub instantly turns your iPad Pro and laptop USB-C port to a 4K HDMI port, 2 USB 3.0 Ports, a USB C charging port with power delivery, 3.5mm Audio/mic 2-in-1 jack (CTIA) and a TF card reader. Plug & Play, with no additional drivers or installation necessary. (NOTE: Only iPad Pro of iOS 13 or above is supported.)

Fast Charge & Data Transfer - The USB-C PD charging port supports pass-through charging of up to 60W, quickly charges iPad Pro while using the other ports, and can connect USB C flash drive for data transfer. USB 3.0 supports transfer speeds up to 5Gbps, fast enough to transfer and share images, files, and videos in seconds.

Crystal Clear 4K Resolution - Mirror or extend your screen with the USB C adapter’s HDMI port and directly stream 4K 3840*2160 @ 30Hz or Full HD 1080p video to HDTV, monitors, or projectors for a cinema-like visual feast. (Tips: Video output for 2018 iPad Pro is 2K@60HZ resolution). And the 3.5mm headphones Jack ensures you talk and listen to music at the same time, keep your focus on call or music.

Pad/Laptop Matching Design - Align the L-shaped holder against the corner of your pad or other USB-C devices, Baseus USB C hub can be securely attached. It is recommended not to wear a protective cover or other accessories that affect the thickness of the device. (such as magic keyboard)

Universal Compatibility Compatible with new iPad Pro 2020/2019/2018, MacBook Pro, MacBook Air 2019/2018, MacBook, Google Chromebook, Dell XPS 13 15, Lenovo Yoga 900, HP Spectre notebook, Surface Book 2, Google Pixel 3 XL/3, Samsung Galaxy Note 10/Note 9/S9/S8/Note 8/, Huawei Mate 30/20/M10/P30/P20, and more USB-C laptop/smartphone.

reviews.title: Don’t buy this knock off, get the real deal
reviews.text: This is the knock-off brand. The real version of this has an SD & micro SD card slot. This version also stopped working for me in under a month. I first noticed it wouldn’t regularly connect to my monitor via the HDMI port, then it began failing to connect all together. The power pass through on this is awful, charging takes forever, but it does with apples dongles also so whatever. Get the real deal & save 50 bucks

reviews.title: I wanted to love this,
reviews.text: I got this for my new iPad. It’s supposed to let you use wired headphones, SIM cards, and even hdmi.  And it does.  It’s a bit cumbersome to use but it does sit on the corner of my iPad even with the iPad cover on.  The big issue i have with it is that it does get hot, VERY HOT, while you’re using it for a longer period of time.  I don’t use it often due to that but i got it at a good price so I’ll keep it.

reviews.title: So far it works as advertised!  Gets alarmingly HOT.. concerned about longevity..
reviews.text:
update::: I've been having issues with connecting this hub to a capture device. it will constantly disconnect and reconnect. I purchased an hdmi splitter that has it's own power source (micro usb) and I connect it with the ipad and the capture device. It's like a middle man... and now the this hub stops disconnecting and reconnecting. It's very odd. Also, connecting power via the USB C port, on the hub, does not help. It's also not a heat issue because I bought a mini fan to blow on it and it's never hot. However, it doesn't disconnect and reconnect when directly connected to a monitor/tv.. it works fine then.

in addition to that issue, I had to get a replacement and noticed that both hubs displayed different color profiles. Using the same monitor, cables, ipad, etc. One was sort of washed out and one was very saturated. Also one wouldn't display 4k (only 2k) and the other one would display 4k, 2k, 1080p, etc. Not really sure why there would be so much variance with the same product.. I guess QC issues?

-----------------------------------

This is the "UPGRADED" version.

So far it works as advertised! Gets alarmingly HOT.. concerned about longevity of the hub itself and concerned about potential damage this could do to my iPad...

This product advertises "Heat vents" but they're just cutouts for the ipad's speakers.. it doesn't actually vent the inside of the hub. This seems deceptive.

I've tried those $20-30 cheap usb c to hdmi + charging port options and they all didn't work properly... mostly they wouldn't actually charge the ipad while outputting the HDMI signal.
This device actually DOES charge while outputting the HDMI signal properly.. which is great.
The audio port works fine, but I have not used the USB ports as I don't really have any use for them at the moment. I mostly needed hdmi output and a charging port that actually charged while hdmi was being used.

I love how the company included 3 different sized spacers to accommodate for ipad's with cases.

The switch to disable the hub is a nice feature. BUT I wish you could disable the charging while still being able to use the HDMI out... so if this switch instead only disabled the charging capabilities but still retained the ability to output video/audio via the HDMI output, that would be, imo, better. Then when the ipad's own battery gets low.. I could flip the switch and allow the hub to start charging. Obviously I can just unplug the USB C charging cable from the hub, but it would be a QoL feature to not have to do that.

I like how it has a long-ish cable instead of just having the usb port extend out of the actual hub body.. this makes it much more versatile, imo.

My biggest concern is how long this product will last due to how HOT it gets while using the HDMI out and charging the ipad at the same time. This is the only time the hub gets alarmingly HOT.. when one is using the HDMI out AND charging at the same time. One could argue that because it's so hot to the touch, that means the passive cooling, inside the hub, is doing its job.. but I don't know if that's actually the case w/o seeing the internals and seeing what kind of heatsinks or w/e are being used. All I know is that this hub gets VERY VERY HOT and devices that overheat tend to stop working sooner than later.

Overall I think this is one of the better options available for 30-50$. The versatility of attaching to the ipad or have it hanging is a huge plus for me.

reviews.title: Convenient & Useful iPad adapter
reviews.text:
So far the only bummer is it’s not really screen protector friendly. I used the thinnest insert because without one it won’t stay in place.

It’s a bit too snug to put on an iPad 11 with a screen protector. The edge of the protector kinda bubbles up. I’ll have to try a few thin cases and not use the inserts to this adapter. That would be more beneficial anyways.

One thing I did notice about the charging port is it’s a bit loose. So you may need to get new cables or try different brands. I have several brands/styles and some do better than others. The Apple cables seem to fit fairly snug though.

Other than that it works as stated. I do like it and it’s more low profile compared to others. All of the ports work especially for charging multiple devices. I use 30w+ for my iPad because I tend to use adapters often. 30w brick & adapter loaded still charged iPad under use.

The adapter looks nice as well. A bit thick but not to the point it’s an issue using it. I have the model with the on/off switch. That’s convenient while otg, in case you need to cut power from the adapter to save the battery from draining.

The adapter doesn’t seem to take a ton of power but if you don’t need it on while away from a power source I’d say just flip it off.

Overall I like this adapter it’s nice & convenient. Like I said my biggest gripe was not really screen protector friendly (iPad w/out a case only screen protector) but it’s not a deal breaker. I’d recommend trying this out!

title: Geekboy Soft TPU Case Cover Compatible for AirPods 3 (2021) with Keychain Portable Shockproof and Dustproof See-Through Protective Cover, Front LED Visible, AirPods 3 Accessories - Clear
features: 【Perfectly Compatible】 The protective cover is specially designed for AirPods 3. It fits perfectly, and the design of the shell and the shell separation makes it very easy to install and remove the protective cover.

【High-Quality TPU Material】 The AirPods 3 case cover is made of high-quality elastomer, Flexible, shock-resistant TPU material, can protect your AirPods case against bumps, slips, drops and scratches.

title: Skullcandy Jib True Wireless Earbud with Microphone / 22 Hour Battery Life / Use with iPhone and Android / Best for Gym, and Gaming / Bluetooth Earbud Headphones - Black/Orange
description: Experience total freedom on a budget with Jib True. The newest true wireless earbud from Skullcandy is easy to connect and easy to enjoy. Call, track change, and volume controls are all within close reach through one main button. 22 hours of audio on a single charge means you spend less time recharging and more time enjoying your music. Jib True earbuds are adventure-ready, too. An IPX4 rating means Jib True is sweat- and dust-resistant. You don’t need to worry about damage if a little rain kicks up during your urban or outdoor adventure. Dual microphones make your calls more clear and if you feel like keeping one ear open, you can use either bud ‘solo’ without missing a beat.
features: Supreme Sound - Powered by 6mm drivers the Jib features dynamic audio with gnarly bass and a wide sound range. The earbuds also feature bluetooth 5.0 for a strong signal making sure you can listen to your music or take phone calls interruption free.

22 Hours of Play - With a 55mAh battery in the earbud and 500 mAh in the charging case you'll get a whopping 22 hours of charge total, 6 in the earbuds and 16 in the case. Use the included charging case for on the go charging as well as safe storage for your earbuds.

Dual Microphones - keeping you sounding extra clear while on calls or talking to your best friend Siri. Dual mics also means you can have 1 ear open and 1 earbud in while on calls to keep aware of your surroundings.

Small, Medium, Large Ears - Included in the Jib is 3 different eargel tips to make sure you have comfy noise isolating fit. The earbuds are super lightweight and water/dust resistant perfect for the active person!
Buy With Confidence - 1 year manufacturer's warranty included with the Jib, be sure to wear your earbuds with full confidence.

reviews.title: OK Headphones
reviews.text: I think these headphones are more like 3.5 stars but I rounded up.  I have kind of a small head for an adult female, and still these headphones were too tight.  Usually I can wear headphones made for kids but not these.  These are definitely for smaller heads and small ears since the ear cups get painful after a while since they aren't big enough to fully cover my ears.  The sound is o.k., nothing to write home about, but for the price (as of this review these head phones cost $19.44) I think they are worth it.  Great for kids, not so good for adults, even if you are a pinhead.

reviews.title: High quality headphones
reviews.text: These are perfect for my 4th grade child to use at school. It is hard to find headphones with plug-ins and that aren't wireless. For my son, the ones with a plug are best to use his school issued ipad. These are sturdy and I'm hoping they last the entire school year.

reviews.title: Not really for large heads.
reviews.text: All the talk about "classroom" and "child's" in the Amazon listing really means "this is a small headset." It's not big enough for me. I have a large (7 3/4) head and this thing actually hurts my ears (presses on them too hard). If you need headphones for a child then this would work just fine. Not for me.

reviews.title: Good headphones for kids
reviews.text: These are perfect for my 6 yr old. They fit her great and don’t get so loud that they can damage her ears.

reviews.title: Quantity over Quality

I would buy again though, just because it's so cheap.

They are fairly easy to connect to bluetooth devices. The case seems to hold a lot of battery and lasts a long time and I like that it charges with USB-C. The ear fins on the earbuds make it very snug and a good fit in my ears, and this also makes it noise cancelling. The case is a nice small size too. I've used it for almost a month and I have no complaints about them :)

reviews.title: Cute Case, but not super functional.

My 1 year old can bust it open and rip apart the holder from the ear pod case. That was disappointing. Also, it stays shut, but would have been nice to have a latch.

reviews.title: Toddler loves them
reviews.text: My daughter loves them. The sound is great without being too loud, the lights are exciting for her and I love that I can turn them off.

reviews.title: Everyone loves these!!
reviews.text: Superb quality and adorable design! I love that it has different modes for the lights, if you don’t want them flashing or want it turned off. We get asked every time she wears them, where we got them from and she gets endless compliments on them. So cute!

reviews.title: Works well.
reviews.text: Bought this for my 8 year old niece.  She loves the headphones.  She says it has great sound quality.

reviews.title: Turns off when I use the Aux
reviews.text: These don’t connect to a number of my Bluetooth devices. Basically only phones and tablets work with them. So I tried to use the AUX cord, and as soon as you turn it on with the aux cord in or plug it in when they’re already on, they shut off. Even in the directions it says they shut off….then why add it??? Even after they shut off, I tried to see if they worked anyway since the directions say that. They don’t. If they only connect to phones & tablets through Bluetooth only, it needs to say that.

reviews.title: Samsung S21
reviews.text: Bought for my Samsung S21 phone. Great sound and feel comfortable.

reviews.title: Excellent backup headphones
reviews.text: The thin wires look fragile, but so far so good. I had forgotten how essential good wired headphones are. I no longer make a fool of myself with audio problems during meetings.

reviews.title: Excellent product
reviews.text: I typically don't care for in-ear headphones but got these so I can talk on my cell while doing chores around the house.  They not only came the same day, but they fit my S21+ perfectly and the sound is great!  I'm so glad I took the plunge and purchased them.  Also love the little case they came with.  Great price for everything.

reviews.title: Phone does not ring in earbuds.
reviews.text: I would have given a 5-star review, but my S20 FE 5G rings out loud for everyone to hear rather than in the earbuds.  I purchased these earbuds to listen to music at the office.  When my phone rings, the volume of the ring matches the volume of the music, which would be fine, except that it's not ringing in the earbuds.  It rings out loud, which disturbs those around me.

reviews.title: Good sound
reviews.text: Not the most comfortable, also tried to use for a call, but it kept disconnecting the call. But I bought them for listening to Audible, so it does the job

title: 2 Pack iPhone Earbuds [Apple MFi Certified] Wired in-Ear Stereo Noise Canceling Isolating Lightning Headphones with Built-in Microphone&Volume Control Compatible with iPhone 13 12 SE 11 X 8 7-All iOS
description:
Using the Step
1.Quick and Easy Connection scheme one:After plugging in the headphones, a window will appear, click "Connect with iCloud" to use.When making or receiving calls, the audio needs to be switched from the speaker to "Baets". 2.Connection scheme two: a.The first time you use it, you need to enter the iPhone settings and turn on Bluetooth. b.Bluetooth page: Find "Baets" in Other Devices, click "Baets" to connect(The headset will automatically connect to your device the next time, so you don’t need to do this step again.) Need Attention: It must be connected to Bluetooth for normal use. And it must always be connected to Bluetooth during use.When making or receiving calls, the audio needs to be switched from the speaker to "Baets".
features: 【Widely Compatibility】: Compatible with all lightning connector devices.Headphone are widely compatible with iPhone 14/14 Pro/14 Pro Max/14 Mini/13/13 Pro/13 Pro Max/13 Mini/12 Mini/12 Pro Max/SE/11 Pro Max/11 Pro/XS Max/X/XR/8/8 Plus/7 Plus/7 Support All iOS Systems.Plug and Play!!!

【Apple MFi certified】: MFi-certified lightning headset, integrated improved C1 chip. Our wired headset offers you the most realistic sound quality with 2 holes that radiate 360° stereo, so you can better immerse yourself in world music during exercise and training environment.

【Mic & Volume Control 】: The headphone for iPhone built-in controller with a high-quality hands-free microphone. Music and Volume controller is designed to play/pause music, volume up/down, next & previous track, answer/end calls Wake up Siri. No need to take out the phone from your bags or pocket when performing these operations,emancipating your hands.

【Easy connection,two ways】:1.The first:After plugging in the headphones, a window will appear, click "Connect with iCloud" to use.When making or receiving calls, the audio needs to be switched from the speaker to "Baets". 2.The second: a.The first time you use it,enter the iPhone settings and turn on Bluetooth. b.Bluetooth page: Find "Baets" in Other Devices, click "Baets" to connect(It will automatically connect to your device next time) Note: It must be connected to Bluetooth for normal use.

【Comfortable Wearing Experience】: The earphones for iPhone with ergonomic angular design and soft & premium ear tips, allow you to listen to the music for a long time without pain perception and keep a long sense of comfort during the wear. If you are not satisfied with this wired Bluetooth earphones, please feel free to contact us.Our service team will reply you with satisfied solution within 12 hours.

reviews.title: Sound quality is just overall dissatisfaction
reviews.text: They are very comfortable but the noice cancellation is slim and the sound quality is just horrible. Sounds like it’s coming from 20 miles away. Shoulda kept with the apple hard ones

reviews.title: Product Is What It Is
reviews.text: The product description stated that this cable would connect your laptop to your television.  I guess it does connect them, if by that you mean an end plugs into both devices.  This cable WILL NOT allow you to watch your laptop on your television.  Instead of buying this, go ahead and buy a converter.  There are several under $20 that work great!

reviews.title: Perfect, and does the job.
reviews.text: Ive had this for a few years now, it fit perfectly and to this day it still doesn't have a scratch on it.
